sense.definition: a choice made or a judgment reached after considering several possibilities
to change one's opinion or decision regarding something

examples.x: George didn't want to come, but we persuaded him to change his mind.
He wasn't going to come, but at the last minute he changed his mind.
If you change your mind about coming tonight, just give me a call.

sense.definition: an act of deciding to choose between two things or more

examples.x: The menu offered a wide choice of dishes.
He was faced with a difficult choice between two careers.
Their choice of music was perfect for the party.
This coat is a great choice for cold weather.
Parents always want the best choices for their children.

sense.definition: to decide what we want to have or what is best for us from a group of options

examples.x: When you go shopping, remember to choose quality over quantity.
I can't decide between these two desserts; you choose for me.
She couldn't choose a favorite book because she loved so many.
We have to choose a destination for our summer vacation.
The chef will choose the best ingredients for tonight's special.

word_list.word: [come] down on one side of the fence or the other

sense.definition: to decide between two possible alternatives or choices that one has

examples.x: In the heated debate over climate change, scientists and policymakers need to come down on one side of the fence or the other to drive effective action.
The committee members couldn't reach a consensus on the proposal, so the chairperson urged them to come down on one side of the fence or the other.
When it comes to choosing a political party, it's important to come down on one side of the fence or the other to actively participate in the democratic process.
The CEO addressed the company's employees, urging them to come down on one side of the fence or the other regarding the new business strategy.
In the contentious legal case, the judge insisted that the jury members carefully consider the evidence and come down on one side of the fence or the other to deliver a fair verdict.

sense.definition: to state that one is bound to do something specific

examples.x: Upon joining the project, team members committed to meeting deadlines and delivering high-quality results.
The CEO publicly committed to donating a percentage of the company's profits to charitable causes each year.
In response to the crisis, the humanitarian organization committed to providing emergency relief and support to affected communities.
Before launching the new initiative, the team committed to conducting thorough research and gathering stakeholder feedback.

sense.definition: to think about something carefully before making a decision or forming an opinion

examples.x: I need to consider whether to accept the promotion.
It's important to consider all the options before choosing.
He carefully considered all the job offers before making a decision.
Before accepting the job offer, he wanted to consider the benefits and drawbacks.
Before purchasing a new car, it's wise to consider factors like fuel efficiency and maintenance costs.

sense.definition: the act or process of discussing something with a person or a group of people

examples.x: She scheduled a consultation with a cardiologist to discuss her heart health.
The company sought a consultation with a financial advisor before making any major investments.
During the initial consultation, the lawyer explained the client's rights and possible outcomes.
Students are encouraged to have a consultation with their professors to discuss their thesis topics.
The IT department held a consultation with the software vendors to address the security issues.

sense.definition: to formally oppose or challenge a decision or a statement

examples.x: She plans to contest the election results in court.
The athlete decided to contest the referee's decision during the game.
They will contest the new policy at the upcoming board meeting.
He intends to contest the charges brought against him in court.
They filed paperwork to contest the patent granted to their competitor.

sense.definition: the particular characteristics that are considered when evaluating something

examples.x: The criteria for choosing the winner of the art contest are creativity and technique.
Her criteria for a good vacation include good weather and interesting activities.
The committee set several criteria for evaluating the effectiveness of the new policy.
The hiring criteria include relevant experience, education, and a positive attitude.
The criteria for this research study include patient age and medical history.

sense.definition: to irrevocably make a decision or to take an action with consequences
to think carefully about different things and choose one of them

examples.x: He had to decide whether to accept the job offer.
After much debate, they decided to go on a road trip.
I couldn't decide between pizza or pasta, so I ordered both.

sense.definition: the act of reaching a choice or judgement after careful consideration

examples.x: The moment of decision had come, and she knew she had to choose between two paths.
The power of decision remained with the shareholders, who ultimately determined the company's policies.
With multiple options on the table, the moment of decision approached for the board of trustees.
In the company, the power of decision rested solely with the CEO, whose word was final.

sense.definition: a person or thing responsible for making important choices or judgments, especially within an organization

examples.x: As the CEO, she is the ultimate decision maker for the company's strategic direction.
The committee members are decision makers in selecting the winner of the scholarship.
In healthcare, doctors are decision makers regarding patient treatment plans.
The board of directors serves as decision makers for approving major investments.
As a parent, you are often the decision maker for your children's upbringing and education.

sense.definition: (mathematics) ‌the study about making the best choice out of available alternatives while considering all the risks and benefits
an official authoritative decision or judgment, especially one made by  a government or the ruler of a country

examples.x: The king issued a decree establishing a new tax law.
The government’s decree mandated stricter environmental regulations.
A royal decree was announced to change the national holiday schedule.
The judge's decree settled the long-standing legal dispute.
The local mayor issued a decree to improve public safety measures.

sense.definition: to make an official judgment, decision, or order

examples.x: The king decreed that all citizens must pay taxes by the end of the month.
The mayor decrees new traffic regulations to improve safety in the city.
The court is decreeing an injunction to halt the construction until further assessment.
The board will decree changes in the company's policies to adapt to the evolving market.
The council decreed new zoning regulations for the residential area.

sense.definition: a predefined option based on which a computer or other device performs a particular task unless it is changed

examples.x: The email client automatically sends messages to the "Spam" folder by default unless the user specifies otherwise.
The default setting for the browser's homepage is the search engine's website, but users can change it to their preferred page.
The smartphone's default wallpaper is a scenic landscape, but users can customize it with their own photos.
The software's default font size is 12 points, but users can adjust it to their preference in the settings.
The default language on the operating system is English, but users can switch to other languages if needed.

sense.definition: someone who is chosen as a representative of a particular community at a conference, meeting, etc.
to think carefully about something and consider it  before making a decision

examples.x: Before accepting the job offer, she took time to deliberate the pros and cons.
It's essential to deliberate the consequences of major financial decisions.
Scientists are attentively deliberating the potential impacts of a new discovery.
She regularly deliberates before making important life choices.

sense.definition: not changing one's decision to do something despite opposition
a situation that is difficult because a choice must be made between two or more options that are equally important

examples.x: She faced a dilemma when she had to choose between attending her best friend's wedding and a critical work presentation.
The doctor was in a dilemma about whether to try a risky procedure or stick with conventional treatment.
The politician's dilemma was whether to support the new policy that was popular but potentially harmful in the long run.
He found himself in a moral dilemma when he discovered his friend was cheating on an exam.
The environmentalists faced a dilemma: support clean energy projects that displaced local communities or oppose them for social justice reasons.

sense.definition: (law) refusal to be bound by a decision or opinion that is contrary to one's beliefs or judgment
to purposefully act slowly

examples.x: She always drags her feet when it comes to completing her assignments, causing delays in the team.
He dragged his feet in making a decision, which resulted in missed opportunities.
They will likely drag their feet on implementing the new policy, causing further frustration among employees.
I'm tired of waiting for him; he keeps dragging his feet on finalizing the contract.
She always drags her heels when it comes to attending meetings, causing frustration among her colleagues.
He dragged his heels in submitting the report, resulting in a missed deadline.
They will likely drag their heels on implementing the new software, causing delays in the project timeline.

sense.definition: to make a decision solely based on throwing a dice, picking a random paper, etc.

examples.x: The finalists in the singing competition had to draw lots to determine the order of their performances.
In order to be fair, the teacher asked the students to draw straws to decide who would present their project first.
The committee members had to draw straws to determine which proposal would receive funding for the community project.
When selecting a winner for the raffle, the organizers decided to draw lots from a hat to ensure a random selection.
